items.headline: U.S. Stocks Open Lower as Geopolitical Unease Returns, Dampening Ceasefire Optimism
items.summary: U.S. stock indices are trending lower on Thursday, retreating from yesterday's significant gains. Initial market confidence spurred by a Middle East ceasefire has been tempered by renewed skepticism and reports of ceasefire violations, leading to a cautious market sentiment. Oil prices have also rebounded as uncertainties persist. [28, 30]

items.statContext: Declined in early trading to approximately $672.93. [28]

items.headline: GameSquare Holdings Surges on Positive Earnings
items.summary: GameSquare Holdings Inc. saw a significant pre-market rally after reporting its first positive adjusted EBITDA in Q4 2025 results and reiterating its 2026 guidance. [13, 28]

items.headline: U.S. Q4 2025 GDP Growth Revised Down to 0.5%
items.summary: The U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis reported that real gross domestic product (GDP) increased at an annual rate of 0.5% in the fourth quarter of 2025, a downward revision of 0.2 percentage points from the second estimate. This growth follows stronger performances earlier in 2025. [11, 21]

items.headline: Federal Reserve Expected to Hold Interest Rates Steady
items.summary: Markets are pricing in a 99.5% likelihood that the Federal Reserve will maintain current interest rates at its upcoming April meeting. The central bank has kept the federal funds rate steady at 3.50% to 3.75% through its 2026 FOMC meetings so far, assessing inflation and employment data. [28, 39]

items.headline: Oil Prices Rebound Amid Middle East Ceasefire Doubts
items.summary: Oil prices climbed back towards $100 a barrel on Thursday, reversing earlier plunges, as skepticism grew over the stability of the U.S.-Iran ceasefire. Benchmark U.S. crude rose 5.4% to $99.44 a barrel, while Brent crude gained 4.1% to $98.70 per barrel, reflecting persistent geopolitical uncertainties. [30]

items.headline: BlackBerry Reports Fiscal Q4 Earnings
items.summary: BlackBerry Ltd. ($BB) announced fiscal fourth-quarter net income of $24.3 million, or 4 cents per share. Adjusted earnings reached 6 cents per share, with revenue for the period totaling $156 million. [24]

items.headline: Workday ($WDAY) Slips Amid AI Disruption Concerns
items.summary: Workday Inc. ($WDAY) saw its stock trend downwards on Wednesday, closing down 6.54% at $119.17. The decline was attributed to investor concerns that advancements in AI could disrupt traditional SaaS business models. [41]
